summary: A dusty soundboard of a high‑energy early‑Dead show with standout Cream Puff War and rare blues gems, captured in a mixed‑quality but cleaned‑up recording.
review: The performance captures the raw excitement of the Dead’s 1966 Matrix gig – Garcia’s solo on Cream Puff War and Pigpen‑driven blues like Death Don't Have No Mercy shine, while a handful of rarities (Betty & Dupree, Big Boy Pete) add historic flavor. The soundboard source is uneven – some copies suffer corruption, pitch errors and hiss, but the cleaned‑up version offers decent balance and enough clarity to enjoy the set.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance (many 4–5 star comments) while noting significant but fixable recording issues; weighted average yields a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Cleaned‑up version reduces hiss, but original SHN files show corruption, pitch errors and occasional drop‑outs. Listeners report needing EQ adjustments; overall balance is decent but clarity is lower than later 66 shows.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A blues‑heavy early set packed with rare treats; while the pacing is energetic, some repetitions make parts feel redundant and the fragmented source can feel disjointed.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s solo on Cream Puff War is described as stellar; his vocals and guitar on Death Don't Have No Mercy are highlighted as a show highlight.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s organ work is noted as a driving force behind the R&B feel of the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy (Kreutzmann) receives praise for an almost “fourth‑arm” performance on several tracks.
